Ron and Marie Stegner
Surface Problem- Financial loss
Deep Problem- Couple is divided and critical of
each other
Words they use are “We feel like roomates.”
Describing Marie, Ron says she does “Piddly
things.”

-- 2 of 18 --

1st Challenge
Physical Stress and Emotional Stress are experienced in
the same way by the body.
The Challenge is to experience physical stress of flying in
fighter jet.
Purpose is to experience stress as something which makes
you stronger. Gives couple feeling of strength and pride in
each other.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 18 --

2nd Challenge
Living on the street and in a homeless shelter in LA.
Husband reprioritizes so that his mission is to care for and protect
his wife. Both make themselves helpful and busy to get through the
week.
They bond together against the hardship and realize what they
have in each other.
Ron says, “I’m not poor, I’m broke.”
Both show the strength of character to treat others as though they
are somebody regardless of their circumstance.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

a plan and to face a fear.
Family shows appreciation for the

has achieved.

work.

excepts challenge to work for Willy, who
is a very unique type of boss.

Key Truths
I have a problem but it is solvable.
My emotional pattern is?
-anger, sadness, anxiety…
I have an optional emotional pattern which is to feel?
-playful, sexy, loving, determined, funny

A Breakthrough is when the
impossible becomes possible
Breakthroughs can look like this:
You have an insight
You become tired of being tired
You step up and change your life by changing your
state, taking action, assigning a new meaning

-- 7 of 18 --

How you deal with stress
determines the quality of your life
Do you use stress or does stress use you?
Stress can either:
Make you grow
Or it can crush you
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 18 --

Suffering is the meaning you
create
Physical pain starts with stressing out, it is just a perception, it
isn’t the facts.
Change what something means and you change your feelings.
When two people are very stressed their relationship is
stressed.
A different problem is one one person feels great and the other
feels badly.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 18 --

Great Life=Great Relationships
Your feelings are most effected by who
you are in a relationship with.
Goal- Bring yourself to a place where you
can bring others up to a higher state.
To do this you must give up blaming of
others, of things, and of yourself.
When in a blame frame of mind you are
unable to have a high quality of life.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 18 --

Choose a meaning and change a
feeling
Giving an empowering meaning instantly transforms how you feel.
What makes people feel crazy is when they have no control.
Remember you always have control over your feelings, state and the
meaning you give.

Triggers for Stress
especially in relationships
Rules
Expectations
Judgments
Shoulds

-- 12 of 18 --

To stop blaming start
questioning
When we experience being upset with another person, we are
judging them.
Relating to the other person releases us from judging them.
You can only believe something that you don’t question.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 18 --

The Work - Exercise
by Byron Katie
4 Questions to ask yourself when you are judging another:
1- Is it true?
2- Make it a statement. Then you stop being upset about it. “He shouldn’t be
angry,” becomes “He is angry.” Can you absolutely know this is true? How
life is supposed to be maybe isn’t the way it actually is.
3- How do you feel when you make the judgments like he shouldn’t, she
shouldn’t? Usually a lot of emotions that make you feel horrible. Tony is
looking at a deep level of how a person feels when they are truthful. On the
surface, they may say “I feel certain” or “I’m right.”
4-What if you didn’t have that thought, like “he is angry”? How would you
feel? Would you just think something else and have a different feeling?
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 18 --

Go Back through the sequence
of questions
Is it true they shouldn’t be that way?
Can you know they aren’t supposed to be that way?
How does it make you feel?
How does it make you feel not to have that thought?
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 18 --

Use the opposite to create a turn
around
Instead of them make it yourself.
“He shouldn’t be angry.” becomes, “I shouldn’t be angry.”
“He is mean.” becomes “I am mean.”
This makes the game change. You are allowed more options.
You are able to relate to the other person. Now, you can choose
to be, who you want to be.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 18 --

Daily Practice
Ask yourself:
How do I feel when I believe this thought?
What if this thought didn’t exist?
What is the opposite?
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 18 --

Write down 5 judgmental
statements you make
Question what you have accepted. Go through the questions
and your answers. Write a letter to the person your judgments
are about. Share your new meaning and your new
breakthrough with them.
Remember:
The quality of your life is the quality of your relationships.
Stress can cause struggle or it can give strength.
